name: Custom Theme
description: Netflix-style skin for Jellyfin: rotating hero carousel, genre rows, top nav tabs, native hover previews, settings panel and 15 fonts. Fully self-contained — the built-in middleware serves index.html with the script injected, so it works on read-only / Docker installs too; the File Transformation plugin is used automatically when installed.
overview: Netflix-inspired skin for Jellyfin with hero, genre rows, nav tabs and a built-in settings panel

versions.changelog: Detail-line fidelity: the critic-rating tomato is hidden (Netflix's metadata line is match/year/duration/maturity only) and the audio/subtitle/version selects are compacted to 420px so the title page reads less like a form.

Card shape 'Portrait' now uses the right artwork: the theme's home genre rows re-render with the poster (Primary) art in a proper 2:3 frame instead of force-cropping the landscape titled boxart (which sliced title art in half). Portrait card widths scale with the Card size setting; landscape/mixed modes keep the titled Thumb boxart as before. Native Jellyfin cards (visible with Clean Home off) are still only reshaped — their image is baked into the markup — and no longer double-apply to the theme's own cards.

Glitch fixes from live use. (1) The home hero billboard now truly starts at the top of the page — the stock home page carries 7.5em of top padding that left a dark band above the hero (scoped to the active home tab so the Favorites tab keeps its normal spacing). (2) Detail-page preview clips now fill the whole backdrop instead of only the top 40vh band (they attach to the full-viewport backdrop container, sit above the rotating backdrop images, and are guarded per item id — in-app navigation between detail pages previously left the old clip running and blocked new ones). (3) With logo art visible, shows no longer display the redundant text title: the original-title line (anime romaji) and the series-name heading on episode/season pages are hidden along with the main title; the episode name stays.

Netflix-fidelity polish. Detail page now shows the title LOGO ART above the metadata line (left-aligned, like Netflix), falling back to the text title when no logo exists or it fails to load (phones/TV keep the text title). Player: red seekbar with red thumb (Live-TV untimed streams keep a clear bar), Netflix-style controls that grow on hover without the circular puddle. 'More Like This' is a static boxart grid instead of a side-scroller (correct 10.11 #similarCollapsible selectors — the old ones were dead, so the 'Similar titles' toggle now actually works too). Episode lists: white titles, clamped synopsis, quiet #404040 separators. Genres restyled as quiet links; maturity badge sharp-cornered; year/duration near-white; synopsis pure white 16px; one consistent weight-500 header voice; header tabs are bare Netflix text instead of pills; theme font now reaches every control (set at html level); action sheets and dialog buttons de-cyaned. MatchScore off now restores the stock star rating instead of a green-number hybrid.

Fixes from live use: (1) Top navigation tabs now use Jellyfin 10.11's actual routes (#/movies, #/tv, #/music, #/list?parentId&serverId) — the removed legacy .html paths made the Collections/list-type tabs spin forever. Active-tab highlighting now distinguishes multiple list-type libraries. (2) New 'Genres never shown as home rows' setting (comma-separated, case-insensitive), excluded before picking the top rows so a full set still renders; defaults to Documentary/Dokumentarfilm/Dokumentation. (3) Home-page detection no longer mistakes a Home Videos library (#/homevideos) for the home page.

Quality and UX overhaul (built on the 2.5.37.2 hotfix). Fixes for every user: admin settings now reach non-admin users (the config is baked into the injected script; the plugin-configuration API requires elevation, so normal users silently fell back to defaults); phones no longer lose the first tap on every card to a popup that is hidden on phones; preview clips on detail pages and a paused hero are capped at the ~30s window instead of streaming (and transcoding) the whole title in a loop; navigating between detail pages no longer leaks a hidden, still-streaming video; the hero pauses in hidden tabs, remembers favorites, keeps its mute icon in sync and caches its slides; library nav tabs for book/photo/mixed libraries now open the right list view; Top-10 ranks land on the theme's own row under Clean Home. Middleware: works behind reverse-proxy Base URLs, coexists with the real File Transformation plugin instead of bypassing it, serves body-less 304s via a content ETag instead of re-sending ~70 KB per load, and no longer corrupts 304/204 responses. The generated CSS now preserves any Custom CSS the admin added (markers), only the selected Google Font family is imported instead of all 15, colors are validated, and uninstalling now removes the injected CSS and script. UI text is localized (EN/DE) end-to-end, hidden-scrollbar rows got Netflix-style hover chevrons and keyboard access, hero dots are real labelled buttons, tooltips show on keyboard focus, prefers-reduced-motion disables autoplay clips and zooms, plus WCAG contrast and 40px touch-target fixes. CSS cleanup: dead/duplicate rules removed, hover zoom honors the Card hover zoom/Animations settings, neighbor-slide no longer shears grid pages, card-size setting now also scales the theme's own rows, huge titles keep the phone downscale, and legacy TVs get aspect-ratio fallbacks. CRITICAL playback fix: with the theme active, inline video in the Jellyfin mobile apps' WebViews was invisible (the theme's opaque backgrounds covered the player, which jellyfin-web renders BEHIND the page and reveals via document transparency; iPhone Safari only worked because it fullscreens video natively). The theme now goes fully transparent during playback, hands the OSD header back to the player (restoring its fade transition so no invisible-but-clickable back button floats over the video, plus safe-area padding on notched phones), keeps its detail-page button styling out of the OSD's own controls, and never applies filters/transforms that would displace the fixed player or OSD bar.

Preview-clip and detail polish, verified live on Jellyfin 10.11.11 + headless browser. (1) Preview clips now play on iOS/iPad and in the mobile app's WebView: iOS only autoplays a <video> whose source answers HTTP byte-range requests (206), which Jellyfin's live transcode stream doesn't - so previews showed 'image zoom but no video' on iPhone while desktop worked. Clips now load the direct original file (static=true, served with 206 + Accept-Ranges) and fall back to an h264 transcode only when the browser can't decode the original (HEVC/MKV) - decided up front from the item's codec via canPlayType, with a no-picture watchdog as safety net, and a final start-0 transcode retry if a seeked remux 500s (MPEG-TS without a keyframe at the seek point). (2) Clips skip the intro like Netflix, starting ~20% into the title, and loop back to that point (not to 0:00). (3) Detail metadata line now leads with the green 'x% Match' (CSS flex order) and drops the player-style 'Ends at HH:MM' estimate Netflix never shows. Hardening: preview videos tear down fully (src detached, watchdogs cancelled) so quick hovers or hero rotation can't leave phantom streams/transcodes running, and the hover preview stops buffering when its 30s window ends.

Netflix-style detail page + a full live UI review, every change verified on Jellyfin 10.11.11 + headless browser. Detail page: (1) the title no longer renders twice - Jellyfin's logo image was positioned off-screen (left:-336px) while the text title showed separately, so the broken logo is hidden and a single bold white title is used, which is also what Netflix falls back to for the many items with no logo art; (2) the Play button is now a proper white Netflix pill with a label ('Abspielen'/'Weiter') instead of a bare icon (Jellyfin stacked the icon over the label in a column; it now lays out as one horizontal row that grows to fit); (3) the title/synopsis block is pulled up so it overlaps the backdrop instead of sitting jammed at the bottom. Review fixes: (4) the login 'Remember Me' checkbox was still Jellyfin cyan (#00a4dc) - it is now a red box with a white check (the cyan was on .checkboxOutline, which the old selector never matched); toggle switches go red too; (5) the hero/hover '% Match' now guards the 0-10 rating range so a stray rating can never render nonsense like '764% Match' (matching the detail-page behaviour). Verified working across home, detail (movie+series), search, library grid and the settings panel.

THE fix release, every change verified on a live Jellyfin 10.11.11 with a real browser. Injection: browsers send Accept-Encoding gzip/br so the middleware saw compressed bytes and never injected (settings button/hero/rows missing in every real browser while curl looked fine) - the index request is now forced to plain HTML; WebPath discovery repaired via IApplicationPaths (the 10.11 host no longer exposes it). Design: hover popup now fires on desktops/VMs (touch detection required any-pointer:coarse), green % Match on detail pages, hero dissolves into the page (no hard color band), Netflix-density rows without under-art text, Netflix-style login box without cyan accents.

CRITICAL injection fixes, found by running a real Jellyfin 10.11.11 + headless browser: (1) browsers send Accept-Encoding gzip/br, so the middleware buffered COMPRESSED bytes and never injected — curl tests passed while every real browser was missing the script (no settings button, no hero, no rows). The index request is now forced to identity encoding. (2) WebPath reflection on the app host finds nothing in 10.11 — now resolved via IApplicationPaths.WebPath, re-enabling direct-serve and on-disk injection. Plus: Housekeeping release: fixed all compiler warnings (nullable annotations in the injection middleware); dashboard settings page now has every setting (CleanHome, GenreRows, NavTabs, TrendingRow were missing); header panel gained Logo letter + Logo image URL fields; removed three dead manifest entries (2.5.12/2.5.15/2.5.16 pointed at releases that never existed, breaking installs of those versions); descriptions and code comments aligned with the current injection strategy (self-contained direct-serve, File Transformation used automatically when present).

Header + Top 10 tweaks from feedback: search, theme and profile now sit at the far right of the header (Netflix layout) instead of bunched next to the nav tabs; the Top 10 row drops the big background rank numbers for a clean poster row.

Two fixes from live testing. (1) Preview clips that 'skipped like a record': the hero and detail clips used the native video loop, which on remuxed MPEG-TS sources restarts at the buffered start before the clip's duration is known — so it strobed the first half-second forever. Clips now play straight through and only re-loop once finished. (2) Navigation drawer: its top entries no longer collide with the logo/tabs (the drawer now clears the fixed header), and while it is open the header goes solid and the page behind dims, Netflix-style.

Header now behaves like Netflix: over the home billboard it is transparent with a soft top scrim at the very top, and fades to solid #141414 as soon as you scroll. Previously it was always solid (the v2.5.29 'header on scroll' note never actually took effect on the home page). Other pages stay solid from the top, as on Netflix.

Closer to Netflix (from a live UI analysis): genre rows now use LANDSCAPE 16:9 boxart cards (Backdrop/Thumb, like Netflix) instead of portrait posters; hover preview gains a Like (👍) button next to Play/+; the hero gets a mute/unmute toggle for its clip; row titles use Netflix's lighter weight; header is more transparent at the top and goes solid (#141414) on scroll.

Fewer clip errors: preview/hero/detail clips now start at the beginning instead of seeking ~2 min in — the mid-file seek failed (HTTP 500) on MPEG-TS files without a clean keyframe there. The hero also rotates a bit slower (20s) to ease the server transcoder. Result: far fewer 500 errors and clips that actually play.

iPad / touch support: on touch devices the hover preview now works by TAP — tap a card once to show the Netflix-style preview popup (it no longer opens the title immediately), tap it again or its Play/More buttons to open. Desktop hover behaviour is unchanged.

Design polish (incorporating ideas from popular Jellyfin themes): frosted-glass settings panel and hover-preview popup (backdrop blur), softer/deeper card-hover shadow, and smoother micro-interactions on the hero/popup action buttons. Visual only — no behaviour change.

Fixes clips that ended on hover / didn't play on the detail page: only ONE clip remux now runs at a time across the whole UI (rotating hero + hover + detail no longer pile up and saturate the server transcoder). The hero also rotates slower (14s) and pauses while you're hovering a card, so a hover/detail clip gets the transcoder to itself and actually plays.

Clip stall watchdog: if a hover/hero/detail clip can't actually start playing within ~10s (a slow or failing on-the-fly remux), it is dropped so the static backdrop/poster image stays — no frozen or endlessly-loading video box. Completes the clip robustness work.

Clip robustness: clips now fade in only once they actually start playing (a stalled/loading clip no longer shows a black box over the backdrop — the image stays). Intro skip is now a small fixed ~2-minute jump (only on longer titles) instead of a deep percentage seek, so the clip starts faster and more reliably.

Preview clips now skip the intro — they start about a quarter into the title instead of at the very beginning (hover, hero and detail). New: opening a title ('Mehr Infos' / detail page) now plays a muted, looping clip of the title over the top backdrop. Top 10 entries you own still open their detail page (playable); the rest open on AniList.

Hero now plays a self-contained autoplay clip of the actual title — a copy-remux of the file itself (not an external YouTube trailer like the Media Bar) — fading in over the backdrop on the active slide; series resolve to a representative episode. Top 10 trending now shows the real global AniList anime hype list (no longer filtered to your library, so it is never empty), ranked 1-10: titles you own link to their detail page, the rest open on AniList.
Loading animation cleanup: removed the black square that showed next to the spinner (the native Material spinner box/background) — now only the centered rotating red ring shows on the loading screen.

Hover preview now actually plays a video (it was showing only the image): the clip is a fast copy-remux to MP4 (h264/aac) instead of a forced downscale transcode — the old params triggered the server's hardware encoder, which failed (ffmpeg 187), and MPEG-TS sources can't direct-play. Series/seasons now stream a representative episode. New loading animation: a centered rotating red ring.

Self-contained injection now works on read-only / Docker web roots too — no File Transformation plugin needed anywhere. The built-in middleware serves index.html itself (reads it and injects in memory) instead of relying on response capture (bypassed by SendFileAsync) or on-disk writes (blocked by a read-only web dir). Enable via the 'Self-contained inject' setting.

Home rows now use Jellyfin's native card markup (title + year text, hover play overlay, data-action delegation) so cards look native and click/play work natively — without fragile minified-bundle patching.

Version consistency fix: meta.json, csproj and manifest are all 2.5.14.0 (the 2.5.13 build shipped meta.json 2.5.12.0, causing a perpetual 'update available'). Same features as 2.5.13 — bundled File Transformation provider (Media Bar works without the separate plugin), self-contained injection, centered loading animation.

Bundled File Transformation: the plugin now provides the File Transformation service itself, so companion plugins like the Media Bar work WITHOUT the separate File Transformation plugin. Plus: the plugin's own injection is fully self-contained (no FT needed). Loading animation centered on screen. New 'Provide File Transformation' setting.

Truly self-contained injection: the plugin now injects its script via its own built-in middleware and no longer asks the File Transformation plugin to do it (no dependency, no double-inject). The File Transformation plugin is only used as a fallback when 'Self-contained inject' is turned off. Fixes the cases where removing/installing File Transformation could break index.html.

Self-contained injection: the plugin now injects its header/hero script into index.html itself via built-in ASP.NET middleware — the File Transformation plugin is no longer required (it stays as an automatic fallback). Defensive (only touches index.html, dedupes, fails open) and can be turned off via the 'Self-contained inject' setting.

Real fix for the wide detail/search/episode pages: Jellyfin's .emby-scroller defaults to overflow:visible (it scrolls via JS) and nothing restored clipping, so card rows expanded to 9000px+. Now forces .emby-scroller to overflow-x:auto globally — rows scroll, pages lay out correctly. Verified live. Home is unaffected (own row containers).

Completes the overflow fix from 2.5.7: the global .verticalSection overflow:visible rule (which the detail/search horizontal scroll containers also carry) was still blowing those pages out to extreme width. Removed it so native scrolling stands — detail, episode and search pages now lay out correctly.

Live-test bug fixes + new features. Fixes: detail/search/episode pages no longer blow out to extreme width (horizontal scrollers were globally forced to overflow:visible) — rows scroll again; the card hover popup poster is now clickable (opens the title); the Netflix 'N' logo navigates home; removed a stray /Users/null/Views 400 before login. New: Netflix-style red loading animation (recolors Jellyfin's spinner, self-contained); a 'Top 10 Anime heute' row built from live AniList trending data, limited to titles you actually have. Both toggleable.

Fix: the Continue Watching ('Weiterschauen') row now shows on the clean home — an older 'hide empty sections' rule was hiding it because its cards use a different class. Home is now hero + Continue Watching + genre rows, all sharp.

Clean Netflix home takeover: the plugin now owns the home page — native/other-plugin rows, empty sections and the page tab bar are hidden, replaced by our hero, a sharp Continue Watching row (no more blurry placeholders) and the genre rows. Fixes the duplicated/overlapping/blurry rows. Toggle off via 'Clean Netflix home' in settings.

Netflix hover-expand popup: hovering a card now floats a larger preview card over the row with a muted autoplay clip, action buttons (Play / + My List / More), title, green % match, maturity rating and genre tags — the signature Netflix card hover.

Hero polish: when a Jellyfin Media Bar is also installed, the built-in hero now sits flush at the top (neutralizes the offset Media Bar pushes onto the home page) so there is no gap or double hero. Smoother crossfade so two slides' titles never overlap during the transition.

Fix: the home page now reliably shows the built-in genre rows and top nav tabs (they were only attempted once on load before the home had rendered). The hero carousel also actively hides a detected Media Bar so there is never a double hero — the built-in Netflix look is now fully self-contained.

Self-contained Netflix experience — no companion plugins needed. New rotating auto-play hero carousel (replaces Media Bar), curated genre rows (replaces Home Screen Sections) and top navigation tabs (replaces Custom Tabs). Fixed blurry/unclickable cards (card image container collapsed to 0px). All features toggleable in the settings panel.

Closer to Netflix: left-aligned header navigation, neighbouring cards slide aside on hover so the expanded card never overlaps, community rating shown as a green 'x% Match', and polished series/episode detail pages (season selector, episode rows, round cast). All toggleable.

Self-contained script injection: the plugin writes the header/hero script into index.html itself (no extra plugin needed), replacing the unreliable middleware. On read-only web dirs it uses the File Transformation plugin if installed. This makes the header settings button and Netflix hero banner appear.

Settings now appear directly in the dashboard menu. Hardened script injection against ChunkLoadError (no-cache on the modified index, 200-only). Netflix design overhaul: hero billboard on the home page, refined card hover, polished detail page, custom scrollbar, English tooltips. Added MIT license and CI. Checksum is filled in automatically by the release workflow.

Complete rewrite. Robust CSS-variable generation (no brittle string replacement), every setting now wired to CSS, single middleware-based script injection (no disk writes), fixed the settings-save event so changes apply immediately, English UI throughout, and all 15 fonts available in the dashboard.
Fix: Replace IHttpResponseBodyFeature to prevent SendFileAsync bypass. Settings button should now work in Docker.
Settings button via IStartupFilter middleware. Works in Docker without volume mount.
Fix: Robust index.html path discovery for Docker. Settings button in header.
Fix: CSS style-break JS injection attempt.
Header settings button, 15 fonts, auto script injection.
Fix settings page load, remove netflix.js, Jellyfin logo default.
Server-side settings. CSS auto-generated from plugin config.
